title: Host libc dependency inventory contract for release-artifact promotion gates
description: Machine-checkable contract for inventorying host glibc dependencies reachable from the interpose release artifact and source-level escape hatches before L1/L2/L3 promotion.

release_artifact_policy.default_behavior: If the release artifact is absent, the checker emits a release_artifact_missing row and still validates the source inventory.
release_artifact_policy.strict_behavior: Set FRANKENLIBC_REQUIRE_RELEASE_ARTIFACT=1 to require target/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so and fail when it is missing.
release_artifact_policy.replacement_blocking_rule: Any remaining host glibc, loader, CRT, startup, dlsym/dlopen, or dynamic runtime dependency is allowed only for L0/L1 interpose unless explicitly approved by a later replacement bead.

artifact_presence_scenarios.claim_effect: source inventory may pass, but L2/L3 standalone claims stay blocked by the explicit release_artifact_missing row
release promotion evidence cannot be claimed without a concrete artifact
dynamic dependencies and undefined symbols are inventoried with readelf/nm evidence

profile_allowlist_policy.real_module_rule: Every non-sentinel allowlist entry must name an existing crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/*.rs module.
profile_allowlist_policy.direct_call_rule: Every source-level libc::function call other than libc::syscall must be in an allowlisted interpose module or a documented resolver module.

negative_claim_tests.unsupported_condition: resolve_host_symbol_raw or resolve_host_symbol_cached remains reachable from the L2 artifact without an allowlisted standalone substitute
release artifact has a dynamic NEEDED entry or undefined symbol requiring host glibc or host loader services
__libc_start_main or __cxa_thread_atexit_impl delegates to host libc outside an explicitly interpose-only path
a source-level libc::function call appears in a module absent from the interpose allowlist and not covered by a documented resolver policy
